name: Add Feature
description: Ship a new feature end-to-end: clarify requirements, design, implement, test, and prepare for release

steps.name: Clarify Requirements
steps.description: Define exactly what the feature does, what it does NOT do, and why. Write acceptance criteria in testable terms. Identify edge cases and states the feature must handle.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Requirements doc with scope, acceptance criteria, edge cases, and out-of-scope items clearly stated

steps.name: Design the Solution
steps.description: Plan the implementation: data model changes, API surface, UI changes, and affected modules. Identify what needs to change and what must stay the same for backward compatibility.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Design doc with implementation plan, list of files to change, and backward compatibility notes

steps.name: Write Failing Tests
steps.description: Write tests that describe the target behavior but fail on current code. This defines done and prevents scope creep. Include unit, integration, and edge case tests.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Tests written that clearly fail on current code and will pass once feature is implemented

steps.name: Implement the Feature
steps.description: Write the production code to make the tests pass. Keep changes focused — no unrelated refactoring. Follow existing patterns and conventions in the codebase.
steps.acceptance_criteria: All tests passing, feature works as specified, no unrelated changes

steps.name: Integration Check
steps.description: Run the full test suite. Manually verify the feature in context. Check that nothing adjacent was broken. Test the happy path and the 2-3 most likely failure modes.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Full test suite passing, feature manually verified, no regressions in related functionality

steps.name: Prepare for Release
steps.description: Update changelog, add migration notes if needed, update relevant documentation, and write a clear commit message summarizing what was added and why.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Changelog updated, docs reflect new feature, migration notes written if applicable
